column3: Test Steps
column4: Expected Result

column2: Verify Successful Login with Valid Credentials
column3: 1. Log in using email and password.  2. Enter a valid email address and password. 3. Click “Login”.
column4: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the main dashboard.

column2: Verify Failed Login with Invalid Credentials
column3: 1. Attempt to log in with an incorrect email address. 2. Attempt to log in with a valid email address and incorrect password. 3. Click “Login”.
column4: Error message displayed indicating incorrect credentials. Login attempt fails.

column3: 1. Log in with a saved email address and password. 2.  Log out. 3.  Log in with the same email address and password.
column4: Remember Me functionality activates.  User is automatically logged in after a defined time (e.g., 30 minutes).

column3: 1. Initiate password reset via email. 2. Receive a reset email. 3. Follow the reset instructions (reset password and email confirmation).
column4: Password reset process is completed successfully.  User receives a new password and confirmation email.

column3: 1. Log in with valid credentials. 2. Attempt to log in with a valid 2FA code (provided via email). 3. Confirm 2FA code.
column4: 2FA is successfully verified and user is logged in.

column3: 1. Enter a malformed email address (e.g., missing @ symbol, incorrect domain). 2. Submit the form.
column4: The application displays an informative error message indicating the email format is invalid.

column2: Test Responsive Design - Mobile Screen Size
column3: 1. Navigate to the login page on a smartphone. 2. Resize the screen to various breakpoints.
column4: The login page adapts correctly to different screen sizes and orientations, maintaining readability and usability.

column3: 1. Load the login page from a mobile device. 2. Measure the time taken to fully load the page.
column4: Page load time should be under 2 seconds.

column3: 1. Navigate to the login page using only the keyboard. 2. Ensure all interactive elements are reachable and operable with a keyboard.
column4: All interactive elements are accessible and navigable using keyboard.

column3: 1. Log in with a standardized login form. 2. Ensure the login page’s visual design aligns with VWO’s established branding guidelines.
column4: All UI elements should match the pre-defined branding guidelines.

column3: Test Steps
column4: Expected Result

column3: 1. Log in with Google credentials. 2.  Check if the user is automatically redirected to the main VWO dashboard.
column4: User is automatically redirected to the main dashboard after SSO.

column2: Test Login Success with User Profile Data
column3: 1. Login as a user with existing VWO profile data. 2. Verify that the user is redirected to the corresponding dashboard page.
column4: Login successful and user is redirected to their defined dashboard.

column2: Test Login Failure - Invalid Session Timeout
column3: 1. Attempt to log in after a configured timeout period. 2. Verify the application displays an error message indicating the session has expired.
column4: Error message displayed indicating session timeout.

column2: Verify Password Reset Flow - Email Validation
column3: 1. Initiate a password reset email. 2. Check for required email field. 3. Confirm email address within the email system.
column4: The password reset process should trigger automatically after successful email verification.

column2: Test Login with Different Browser Types
column3: 1. Attempt to log in using Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
column4: Login should work consistently across all supported browsers.

column3: 1. Enable Mobile Login. 2. Ensure Login is accessible on mobile device.
column4: Mobile login should function properly.

column3: Test Steps
column4: Expected Result

column3: 1. Initiate the "Forgot Password" process. 2. Receive email with a link to reset the password. 3. Click the link to reset the password.
column4: The user successfully resets the password via email and is redirected to the new password.

column2: Test User Flow with Large Amount of Data
column3: 1. User provides extremely large data through form. 2. Ensure the UI handles large data efficiently.
column4: The application can handle the data volume without visual impact or performance degradation.

column3: 1. Perform Login with Dark Mode enabled
column4: 2. Verify login page functionality across dark mode.
column5: Login Page functionality works as expected.

column2: Test User Flow - Multiple User Accounts
column3: 1. Log in with multiple user accounts. 2. Verify switching between users.
column4: All user accounts should function according to expected behaviour.

column3: Test Scenario
column4: Expected Result

column3: 1. Measure the time taken to load the login page.
column4: Login Page load time must be below 2 seconds.

column3: 1. Simulate a brute force attack on the login form.
column4: The application must quickly detect and reject malicious attempts.

column3: 1. Introduce a weak password. 2. Verify that the application enforces password complexity rules.
column4: Password complexity rules must be enforced.

column3: 1. Verify that all interactive elements are accessible with keyboard navigation.
column4: All interactive elements are easily navigable using keyboard.

column3: 1. Verify the data encryption algorithm used during login.
column4: Ensure data is encrypted during transmission.

column3: 1. Simulate several failed login attempts in a short period.
column4: The application should not record any failed login attempts.

column3: 1. Simulate many login requests simultaneously.
column4: Login service should remain responsive under high traffic.

column3: 1. Simulate API call response time.
column4: API Call Response time below threshold.

column3: Test Steps
column4: Expected Result

column3: 1. Test login with a crafted SQL injection.
column4: The application should prevent the successful login attempt.

column3: 1.  Submit form containing malicious script.
column4: The application should block the script.

column3: 1.  Attempt to impersonate a valid user.
column4: The application should prevent session hijacking.

column3: 1. Simulate scenarios for the application error messages.
column4: The application display understandable error messages.

column3: 1. Simulate many failed login attempts.
column4: The application should not count failed logins.

column3: 1. Simulate many API requests.
column4: API response time below threshold.
